mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears and force equity portfolio de-risking.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer spending and construction activity, driving corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 58%.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply chain bottlenecks and falling factory output, prompting short-term equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns with a win rate exceeding 60% during supply shock periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating core inflation force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and suppressing equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases while CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3%
failure_condition: The joint signal of rate hikes and inflation acceleration fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a win rate above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer purchasing power, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive equity returns over three months.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms strong economic momentum that boosts investor confidence and triggers immediate short-term equity buying pressure.
failure_condition: GDP growth above the 2.5 percent threshold does not produce positive 5-day forward returns in at least 60 percent of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising employment indicates inefficient capacity utilization and margin compression, leading to equity depreciation over one month.
failure_condition: The production-employment divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a win rate above 57% during stagflationary periods.
